design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat encounters with progressive character leveling: defeat enemies → gain EXP → unlock moves and stat increases → tackle harder stages. Encouraged by rewarding finishing moves with higher EXP values.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Single-player combat against escalating enemy difficulty offset by gradual power progression system; risk/reward of using stronger finishing moves (more EXP) vs. defensive play.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as iconic Final Fight characters (Haggar, Cody, Guy) in a playful, 'super deformed' comedic take on the arcade classic; rescuing the Mayor's daughter through street brawling.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate tonal shift to chibi/cartoony comedy (like Kid Dracula and Splatterhouse: Wanpaku Graffiti) rather than serious action; adoption of Double Dragon's EXP system for NES beat 'em up genre; single-player only (removing arcade's co-op) while keeping all three characters. Satirical plot (e.g., Belger wanting to marry Jessica rather than ransom her).

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives available as power-up items; player character has vitality bar that depletes on damage
difficulty.continues_system: Additional continues available as power-up drops; Abigail boss fight in Japanese version offers bonus continue via trivia quiz (removed in western versions)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; five main stages plus two bonus rounds. Power-ups and character leveling carry between stages.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via leveling system: players accumulate EXP at variable rates depending on finishing move choice, directly increasing attack power and max vitality. Starting levels vary by character (Cody/Guy at Level 1, Haggar at Level 3), allowing difficulty tuning through character selection.

progression.upgrade_system: EXP-based leveling system with six skill levels total. Level progression increases attack power and maximum vitality. At Level 4, each character unlocks a signature special move: Cody gains Tornado Sweep, Guy gets Tornado Kick, Haggar receives Running Clothesline.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Experience points earned from defeating enemies (stronger finishing moves yield more EXP). New special attack moves unlock at Level 4 threshold. Character-specific weapons (knife for Cody, shurikens for Guy, mallet for Haggar) appear as stage pickups.
replayability_hooks: Three distinct playable characters with different fighting styles and unlocked special moves
Character choice affects starting difficulty (Haggar begins at Level 3 vs. Level 1 for others)
Finishing move selection impacts EXP gains, encouraging varied combat approaches
Two bonus rounds add content beyond five main stages

world_building_techniques: Comedic reinterpretation of original Final Fight arcade plot (kidnapped Mayor's daughter Jessica)
Satirical dialogue exchanges with bosses (e.g., Damnd's boastful taunts in Stage 1; Abigail's trivia quiz in Japanese version)
Regional dialogue variations create localization flavor (Japanese version includes third prompt to Damnd and full trivia sequence; western versions strip these)
Character-specific instruction manual descriptions establish personality and martial disciplines
Visual chibi art style conveys tone shift toward parody/comedy

boss_design.philosophy: Childlike counterparts of Mad Gear Gang members from original arcade game; each boss functions as stage climax with dialogue/character interaction. Comic tone allows satirical reinterpretations (e.g., Belger recast as lovestruck antagonist).
notable_bosses: Thrasher (Stage 1) - Damnd counterpart; can lose health via dialogue prompts if player answers correctly
Abigail (Stage 3) - Boss who offers optional trivia quiz in Japanese version for bonus continue
Cyborg Belger (Stage 5/Final) - Mechanically enhanced version of original main antagonist; represents escalation from standard bosses

faction_indicators: Mad Gear Gang serves as unified antagonist faction across all stages
Three playable character classes (brawler/Cody, ninja/Guy, wrestler/Haggar) represent different combat philosophies within hero team
Mayor Haggar's authority position vs. street gang opposition creates civic order tension
danger_signals: Kidnapping of Jessica (damsel-in-distress premise) catalyzes conflict
Stage progression through increasingly dangerous gang territory (Mad Gear strongholds)
Final boss escalation (Cyborg Belger) signals existential threat elevation
environmental_storytelling: Five distinct stage locations imply Metro City geography and gang territorial control
Chibi/cartoonish enemy designs and environments contrast comedic tone against serious kidnapping plot
Weapon pickups (knives, shurikens, mallets) reflect character archetypes and stage dangers
Japanese version's Statue of Liberty posters in Stage 1 (per trivia question) establish urban American setting flavor
